untranslatable_reason: Signal requires manual peak/trough identification and channel drawing (geometric pattern recognition). This cannot be expressed using only the allowed indicators and operators. Peak detection requires lookback logic with comparisons like high[i] > high[i-1] AND high[i] > high[i+1], which violates the no-array-indexing rule. Donchian channels (dc_upper/dc_lower) are provided as a proxy for support/resistance levels but do not capture the specific 'two-peak channel' construction described in the original signal.
translation_notes: KAUFMAN_DRY_14 is a pattern-based Elliott Wave signal requiring subjective peak identification and manual trendline construction. Backtesting would require custom peak-detection logic not supported by the current indicator set. Donchian channels offered as alternative for channel-based exit conditions. Signal is direction-agnostic and context-dependent on wave structure.
